stem: Let \( f(x,y) = \dfrac{[[A]]xy}{x^{2}+y^{2}} \) for \( (x,y) \ne (0,0) \). What is \( \displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0} f\!\left(x, \dfrac{[[m_num]]}{[[m_den]]}x\right) \), the limit of \( f \) as \( (x,y)\to(0,0) \) along the line \( y = \dfrac{[[m_num]]}{[[m_den]]}x \)?
solution: Substituting \( y = mx \) with \( m = \dfrac{[[m_num]]}{[[m_den]]} \) gives \( f(x,mx) = \dfrac{[[A]]mx^{2}}{x^{2}(1+m^{2})} = \dfrac{[[A]]m}{1+m^{2}} \) for \( x\ne 0 \), a constant independent of \( x \), so the limit as \( x\to 0 \) is \( \dfrac{[[A]]m}{1+m^{2}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the limit is always \( 0 \), which only happens for the special slope \( m=0 \).
This reports \( A \) alone, dropping the dependence on the slope \( m \).
This drops the \( 1+m^{2} \) denominator that comes from \( x^{2}+y^{2} \).
This drops the coefficient \( A \) entirely.
This is off by one from the correct limit along this line.
This is off by one from the correct limit along this line.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = \dfrac{[[A]]x^{2}y}{x^{4}+y^{2}} \) for \( (x,y) \ne (0,0) \). What is \( \displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0} f\!\left(x, \dfrac{[[k_num]]}{[[k_den]]}x^{2}\right) \), the limit of \( f \) as \( (x,y)\to(0,0) \) along the parabola \( y = \dfrac{[[k_num]]}{[[k_den]]}x^{2} \)?
solution: Substituting \( y = kx^{2} \) with \( k = \dfrac{[[k_num]]}{[[k_den]]} \) gives \( f(x,kx^{2}) = \dfrac{[[A]]kx^{4}}{x^{4}(1+k^{2})} = \dfrac{[[A]]k}{1+k^{2}} \) for \( x\ne 0 \), a constant independent of \( x \), so the limit as \( x\to 0 \) is \( \dfrac{[[A]]k}{1+k^{2}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the limit along every path through the origin is \( 0 \), which is false for this function.
This reports \( A \) alone, dropping the dependence on \( k \).
This drops the \( 1+k^{2} \) denominator that comes from \( x^{4}+y^{2} \).
This reports \( k \) alone, dropping the coefficient \( A \).
This is off by one from the correct limit along this parabola.
This is off by one from the correct limit along this parabola.
